items.title: Egypt receive a warm welcome home
items.description: Watch as the Egyptian football team return to a hero's welcome from their fans.
items.titleIt: Egypt receive a warm welcome home

items.title: Somalia: Ambassador Daccò Coppi Meets with the Minister of Information
items.description: (ANSA) - NAIROBI, 08 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Somalia, Pier Mario Daccò Coppi, met with the Somali Minister of Information, Culture, and Tourism, Abdifatah Qasim, with whom he visited the National Theater and the headquarters of Somali state radio and television. The National Theater is a building dating back to the 1960s that retains considerable architectural value within Mogadishu’s urban landscape and serves as a cultural landmark that the authorities hope to revitalize and promote after more than 35 years of inactivity. Minister Qasim emphasized the importance of cultural promotion
items.titleIt: Somalia: Ambassador Daccò Coppi Meets with the Minister of Information

items.title: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab
items.description: The Somali government’s battle with Al-Shabaab militants grinds on, with neither able to gain a decisive advantage. To improve its lot, Mogadishu needs to bolster its military and alleviate civilian suffering in areas under insurgent control, while looking for openings for dialogue with the group.
items.titleIt: New Chapter, Same Stalemate: Somalia’s War with Al-Shabaab

items.title: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran
items.description: Egypt has been walking a tightrope since the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran began, joining mediation efforts while signalling solidarity with Gulf Arab partners under Iranian attack. But with some Gulf capitals voicing expectations of stronger support, Cairo may find itself under increasing pressure.
items.titleIt: “Strategic Balance” under Stress: Egypt and the U.S.-Israeli War with Iran

items.title: Kenya: World Population Day - Renewing Our Promise to Every Woman and Every Newborn Everywhere
items.description: [allAfrica] On World Population Day, we are often asked to look at numbers such as how many people we are, how fast we are growing, and what our population will need in the years ahead. They should help us understand the conditions into which people are born, the systems that shape their health and opportunity, and the futures they are able to build.
items.titleIt: Kenya: World Population Day - Renewing Our Promise to Every Woman and Every Newborn Everywhere

items.title: Algeria: First ATR Aircraft for Domestic Flights Delivered
items.description: (ANSA) - ALGERI, 12 LUG - The Italian Ambassador to Algeria, Alessandra Schiavo, attended yesterday’s handover ceremony for the first of the 16 ATR 72-600s ordered by the national carrier Air Algérie. The aircraft is intended for domestic flights and will be operated by the new airline Domestic Airlines, a subsidiary of Air Algérie. In her remarks, as reported by the Italian Embassy in Algiers on its social media channels, Schiavo emphasized that Italian industrial excellence is once again at the heart of a strategic project for Algeria, which will help enhance regional connectivity and local
items.titleIt: Algeria: First ATR Aircraft for Domestic Flights Delivered
